result:
If I could choose one change for the world, it would be to foster a collective, unshakable empathy — a world where every person consistently understands and feels the joys, pains, hopes, and dignity of others as if they were their own. Not as sentiment, but as a lens through which all decisions — personal, political, economic, and environmental — are made.

Why this?  
Because so much of what harms the world stems from a deficit of felt connection: inequality, conflict, indifference to suffering, exploitation of people and planet. If empathy were woven into the fabric of human consciousness, systems would naturally bend toward justice, compassion, and sustainability. It wouldn’t erase all differences or challenges, but it would transform how we navigate them — from separation to shared humanity.
